name: ASAP Transport
recent_news: BBB profile indicates business is no longer in business (as of 2026 listing). Founded 10/6/2008 in Loves Park, IL.
fit_notes: Not a tuck-in candidate: BBB indicates company is out of business. Micro single-vehicle operation in Rockford-area; even if active, too small to move the needle.

name: Valley Care Transport
recent_news: Veteran-owned NEMT van service; active 2024-2026 per Birdeye/BBB/YouTube marketing. No school-bus or school-district contracts identified.
fit_notes: Not a Summit fit: this is a non-emergency medical transport (NEMT) wheelchair-van operator, not a school-bus contractor. Single van, veteran-owned solo operator in Shenandoah Valley.

name: Corporate Shuttle Svc
recent_news: No active website or news traces found; owner Virgil V. Palaypay (New Castle, DE, b. 1945) is the only public link. Listing classifies as school but search results suggest corporate/employee shuttle focus, not contract school-bus service.
fit_notes: Likely not a school-bus tuck-in: name and limited footprint suggest corporate/employee shuttle, not yellow-bus district contractor. Owner is ~80+ so succession theoretically present, but scale (~7 employees, PO Box) and lack of school-district trace make it unattractive.

name: Lion's School Shuttle Svc
districts_or_customers: Inland Empire / Redlands-Riverside area (Redlands USD market)
recent_news: FMCSA SAFER snapshot lists Lions School Shuttle Services Inc (USDOT 2910683, MC-980550) with 2 power units and 4 drivers; passenger authority active. Second USDOT 2626124 also tied to entity. No M&A activity.
fit_notes: Not a viable tuck-in: confirmed independent but only 2 buses / 4 drivers — sub-scale micro-carrier in Redlands. Could be a route-cluster pickup if Summit ever wants Inland Empire density, but not material on its own.

name: Mabel School Trnsprtn Svc Inc
recent_news: No web presence, no phone listed, NOT on the published M-DCPS approved private bus companies list (2025). Likely dormant or sub-contracts to other Miami-Dade operators.
fit_notes: Not a tuck-in candidate: micro single-bus operator (2 employees, no phone, no website) not on M-DCPS approved list. Even in the fragmented Miami-Dade private school-bus market, this is too small/inactive to pursue.
